title: Line 47900 – Provincial or territorial credits - Canada.ca
content:
## Line 47900 – Provincial or territorial credits
Select your province or territory of residence on December 31, 2025 , to find out which amount you may be able to claim and which form you should complete.
- Alberta Complete Form T89 to calculate your Alberta stock savings plan tax credit . Enter the amount on line 47900 of your return. Attach a copy of this form to your paper return.
Complete Form T89 to calculate your Alberta stock savings plan tax credit . Enter the amount on line 47900 of your return.
Attach a copy of this form to your paper return.
- New Brunswick Complete Schedule NB(S12) to calculate your New Brunswick seniors' home renovation tax credit . Enter the amount on line 47900 of your return. Attach a copy of this form to your paper return.
Complete Schedule NB(S12) to calculate your New Brunswick seniors' home renovation tax credit . Enter the amount on line 47900 of your return.
Attach a copy of this form to your paper return.
- Prince Edward Island Complete Form PE428 to calculate your Prince Edward Island volunteer firefighter and volunteer search and rescue personnel tax credit . Enter the amount on line 47900 of your return.
Complete Form PE428 to calculate your Prince Edward Island volunteer firefighter and volunteer search and rescue personnel tax credit . Enter the amount on line 47900 of your return.
- Quebec Complete the Quebec income tax return to claim your provincial credits.
Complete the Quebec income tax return to claim your provincial credits.
- All other provinces and territories For residents of British Columbia, Manitoba, Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, Northwest Territories, Nunavut, Ontario, Saskatchewan, or Yukon: Complete Form 479 to calculate your provincial or territorial credits and enter the amount on line 47900 of your return. Attach a copy of this form to your paper return.
For residents of British Columbia, Manitoba, Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, Northwest Territories, Nunavut, Ontario, Saskatchewan, or Yukon:
Complete Form 479 to calculate your provincial or territorial credits and enter the amount on line 47900 of your return.
Attach a copy of this form to your paper return.
